title: Unsafe Best Practices and Review Checklist
content:
Writing unsafe Rust well requires discipline. The goal is to confine unsafety to the smallest possible region, document all invariants, test thoroughly with Miri, and provide safe public APIs.

Think of unsafe blocks like a security boundary: every unsafe block should have a clear reason for existing, documented safety invariants, and ideally a safe wrapper that external code uses instead.

codeExamples.description: Document safety invariants with SAFETY comments
Safe public API wrapping unsafe internals

keyPoints: Minimize unsafe blocks: only include the operations that require unsafe
Add # Safety doc sections on every unsafe function explaining preconditions
Add // SAFETY: comments on every unsafe block explaining why invariants hold
Provide safe public APIs that wrap unsafe internals
Test with Miri regularly: cargo +nightly miri test
Follow the review checklist: scope, docs, pointers, lifetimes, thread safety
If you can achieve the same result with safe code, do not use unsafe
